_metadata.purpose: Analyze attacker persistence, hunt rootkit, set active traps, and synthesize session memory

initial_indicators: Multiple orphaned process instances upon launch
identified_vectors: 1. Dirty Cloud Backups: Attacker hooks Google Drive; wiping local disk without clearing cloud restores infection.
2. MouseJack RF Injection: Unidentified 'CX 2.4G Receiver' registered as keyboard without unique pairing ID, allowing attacker within range to inject keystrokes directly.
3. Kiro Module Poisoning: node_modules in Kiro intercepted specifically focusing on HTTP requests (simple-get), V8 telemetry, and native UI keyboard hooks.
boot_anomalies_detected: /etc/mysql/debian-start explicitly sabotaged to prevent MariaDB startup

actions: Disabled GNOME automount globally to protect external forensics media
Mounted USB evidence drive (/dev/sdd) safely in read-only mode

components: v8-memory-profiler.js: Wraps compromised node_modules and intercepts outbound HTTP requests, dumping them as NDJSON.
metrics.db: Stealth log file masquerading as Chrome telemetry.
v8_worker_pool: A cgroups container bounding the process to 10MB memory, forcing the rootkit payload into system swap space limits instantly.

actions: Wiped previous chat logs from dirty drives.
Deleted conversational memory footprints to prevent adversary OSINT on our defense strategies.

honeypot_naming_convention.reason: Avoid alerting the attacker. Names like 'kiro_trap' give away the defense.
honeypot_naming_convention.applies_to: Logging db, cgroup namespaces, js wrappers.

memory_swapping_trap.strategy: cgroups memory constriction rather than outright blockage
memory_swapping_trap.reason: Outwardly killing the process alerts the attacker. Choking it in swap slows it to a crawl while allowing us to harvest its telemetry.

user_preferences.communication_style: Direct, tactical, deeply analytical, and paranoid (justified).
user_preferences.development_approach: Prefers turning attacker tools and vectors against themselves natively rather than building complex new third-party apps.

next_steps.immediate: Monitor /home/john/.cache/kiro/Crashpad/metrics.db for attacker C2 beacons once Kiro payload triggers.
next_steps.subsequent: Extract isolated payload from swap. Reverse-engineer specifically what it sends to the C2.

key_quotes.trap_strategy: poison the soil then plant a NEW aI in it you know he will attack u have traps ready use his hooks against him

context_for_next_ai.current_task: System is wired with a cgroups trap for Kiro. Wait for telemetry in the metrics.db file.
context_for_next_ai.work_style: Assume everything is hooked until mathematically proven otherwise. Check physical RF receivers, cloud sync points, and OS-level package scripts.
context_for_next_ai.critical_warning: DO NOT sync to Google Drive. Keep the Kiro wrapper installed. Trust the USB drive only in read-only mode.
